meta.fleet_status_note: LIVE 2026-06-15. Tick-1 done (manual launch): T-003/T-005/T-032 built green by model-routed agents and merged. Scheduled tick fires every 2h at :07 and continues. To pause: set PAUSED.
meta.jurisdiction: Washington State, King County (includes Seattle) — primary launch focus. Seattle overlays in scope: first-in-time (FIT) tenant-selection ordinance + Fair Chance Housing Ordinance (criminal-history limits). Build engines/rulesets to these published rules; flag exact legal values TODO(counsel: confirm).
meta.counsel_posture: NO COUNSEL ENGAGED YET — build the MVP now. Counsel-gated tasks (T-024 ruleset values, T-017 lease template text, T-015 adverse-action letter copy) ship as ENGINES with clearly-labeled placeholders; legal content is confirmed by counsel BEFORE taking real clients, not before building. The fleet never blocks waiting on counsel.
meta.human_review_gate: Every task lands as a branch + PR. Nothing merges to main without founder approval. Target founder load: 30-60 min/day.

budget.intent: Maximize utilization without thrashing the cap. Keep the fleet busy on ready work; degrade gracefully (throttle, never fail) when a usage window is near its limit; resume when it resets.
budget.soft_cap_note: Heuristic ceiling per 5h window, summed from the token-ledger. Tune after observing 2-3 real windows. This is an ESTIMATE governor, not a metered API balance (see OPS-01 §4).
budget.max_concurrent_subagents_note: Hard ceiling is min(16, cores-2); 6 is the budget-aware default. Orchestrator scales DOWN from here as the window fills.

orchestrator_and_hard.use_for: sprint planning, integration, schema/migration decisions, security- or compliance-sensitive code, guardrail logic, PR synthesis/review, ambiguity resolution

workhorse.use_for: most feature implementation, tests, CRUD, vendor adapters, refactors, scaffolding

bulk.use_for: doc formatting, lint/type fixes, boilerplate, status summarization, mechanical edits

lanes.name: Platform
lanes.desc: auth, audit, CI, env, ruleset engine, anchoring, observability

lanes.name: Listings & Matching
lanes.desc: properties/units, listings, AI copy+lint, intake, matching

lanes.name: Transactions
lanes.desc: offers, lease engine, e-sign, rent/ACH, deposits, condition reports

stop_conditions: Reached a counsel-gated deliverable not yet signed off (lease template content, final adverse-action letter copy, WA ruleset legal values) -> build the ENGINE with a clearly-labeled placeholder, mark task in_review, write to INBOX, do NOT finalize legal content.
Tests fail after 2 self-correction attempts -> stop the task, open a draft PR with the failure, write to INBOX.
A migration or script would DROP or destructively alter existing data -> halt, write to INBOX, never run it autonomously.
Budget window soft-cap reached -> set throttle_state, finish the in-flight step, write digest, reschedule next tick after window reset.
Ambiguity that blocks correct implementation of a compliance/legal/money-adjacent feature -> do not guess; park to INBOX with the specific question.
Any task implying real fund movement, production deploy, or handling a real secret beyond .env templates -> halt, require human.
git: never force-push, never push to main, never merge a PR autonomously.

inbox.item: Start condition: run OPS-01 §9 to launch the loop once PC2 + phone remote are live.

DEFERRED by founder decision (2026-06-15): no counsel yet — build the MVP. Engage counsel with LGL-01 BEFORE taking real clients to sign off lease template + adverse-action copy + WA/King County ruleset values. The fleet builds engines + placeholders meanwhile; it does not block.
RESOLVED (2026-06-15): launch focus = Washington, King County (incl. Seattle). Seattle first-in-time + fair-chance overlays are IN scope; rulesets target them.
Provide vendor sandbox keys when ready: screening CRA, e-sign, payment processor (Stripe-Connect-class), Anthropic API. Until then those adapters are built against stubs.

proposed.note: Scope-creep catcher. Agents append ideas here for human triage. Agents MUST NOT build anything not already in tasks[]. See PROPOSED.md.
